Ned is all appetite and action, the earthbound counterweight to Nemo's philosophical withdrawal. Blunt and unsociable, he nonetheless took a liking to the narrator (TTLU-004-011), and his loyalty to his companions runs beneath his gruffness. His prowess as the King of Harpooners (TTLU-004-007) marks him as a man of the surface world — of hunt, air, and open horizon — who can never be reconciled to life below.
description: 'The daring French adventurer who volunteers to ride the projectile to the Moon.'

The Eighty-Day Wager is the bet that sets *Around the World in Eighty Days* in motion. During an evening of whist at the Reform Club, a dispute over whether the globe can be circled in a fixed time prompts Phileas Fogg to stake his fortune on the claim, "In eighty days" (AWED-003-027).

Ecuador is the equatorial South American republic whose frontier the Amazon defines in *Eight Hundred Leagues on the Amazon* — the village of Iquitos lies on that portion of the great river whose bed separates Peru from the republic of Ecuador. (EHLA-003-001)

Tiflis is the chief city of Transcaucasia and the gateway from which the great Asian journey begins. It was there, on the 13th of May, that the correspondent found the telegram redirecting his travels. (ASC-001-005) The station at Tiflis is the junction of three lines of railway, ending west at Poti on the Black Sea and east at Baku on the Caspian. (ASC-001-019)
description: The great Mediterranean island, also called Candia, in rebellion against the Ottoman Empire as the Nautilus passed its waters.

description: 'The bleak sub-Arctic coast of northeastern Canada, a waymark of the northern voyages and namesake of tundra plant and stone.'

Vancouver Island marks the bend of the northwestern American coast. Verne notes how "the coast curves off from Vancouver Island up to the Aleutians" (RC-011-003), the isle standing at the southern end of the long arc that sweeps toward the [[Aleutian Islands]] and the Russian sphere ceded to the United States in 1867.
